diagrams.title: Cloud Deployment Models Overview
diagrams.description: A comparative visual representation of the four cloud deployment models: Public, Private, Hybrid, and Community clouds, showing their ownership, access, and infrastructure scope side by side for easy comparison.
elements: Public Cloud icon (multi-tenant, shared resources, third-party provider)
Private Cloud icon (single-tenant, dedicated resources, organization-owned or leased)
Hybrid Cloud icon (combination of public and private, interconnected)
Community Cloud icon (shared by organizations with common concerns)
Comparison axes: Ownership, Access, Infrastructure, Cost, Security level
diagrams.purpose: Introduces and compares all four cloud deployment models, helping learners understand the fundamental differences in ownership, access model, and infrastructure scope.

diagrams.title: Hybrid Cloud Architecture Diagram
diagrams.description: A detailed architectural diagram showing how a Hybrid Cloud environment connects on-premises infrastructure, private cloud, and public cloud services through a network, with data and application flow arrows indicating how workloads move between environments.
elements: Public Cloud provider (AWS, Azure, GCP icons)
Workload placement indicators (sensitive vs. scalable workloads)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Hybrid Cloud architecture concept, demonstrating how organizations combine multiple cloud types to optimize cost, performance, and security while enabling workload portability.

diagrams.title: Community Cloud Shared Infrastructure
diagrams.description: A diagram illustrating how multiple organizations with similar requirements (e.g., government agencies, healthcare institutions, financial organizations) share a community cloud infrastructure, showing the shared responsibility model and common policies.
elements: Multiple organization icons (Org A, Org B, Org C)
Shared Community Cloud infrastructure (shared servers, storage, network)
Common security policies and compliance requirements
Dedicated partition for each organization within shared infrastructure
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the Community Cloud model, showing how organizations with shared interests collaborate to build and maintain cloud infrastructure that meets their collective security, compliance, and regulatory requirements.
